com.pax.poslink

Class PaymentRequest.TransactionBehavior

  • java.lang.Object
    • com.pax.poslink.PaymentRequest.TransactionBehavior
  • Enclosing class:
    PaymentRequest

    public static class PaymentRequest.TransactionBehavior
extends java.lang.Object

    • Field Summary

      Fields 
      Modifier and Type Field and Description
      java.lang.String AcceptedCardType
      The card type bitmap, refer to Card Type Bitmap definition.
      It makes POS system available to control which card types accepted for current command.
      java.lang.String AccessibilityPinPad
      The standard of accessibility to support and the status and visibility of “Accessibility Switch” on the Input Account screen.
      java.lang.String AddlRspDataFlag
      Additional response data request flag;
      java.lang.String CardPresentMode
      Card Present mode for current command:
      java.lang.String CoFIndicator
      Indicates if the transaction is Credential/Card on File (CoF) transaction.
      java.lang.String CoFInitiator
      Indicates if the credential/card on file (CoF) transaction is customer initiated or merchant initiated.
      java.lang.String DebitNetwork
      Indicate which debit network to use.
      java.lang.String DistransPrompts
      8 digits bitmap for disabling transaction prompts.
      java.lang.String EntryMode
      The entry mode bitmap, refer to Entry Mode Bitmap Definition.
      It makes POS system available to control which entry mode accepted for current command.
      java.lang.String ForceCC
      Force terminal to do commercial transaction flag:
      java.lang.String ForceDuplicate
      Duplicate transaction override flag, if this flag is set to ‘1’ the host will not check for duplicate, also it will override the local duplicate check.
      java.lang.String ForceFsa
      Force terminal to do FSA transaction flag:
      java.lang.String GiftCardIndicator
      Gift card indicator indicates whether AMEX transactions contain a gift card purchase.
      java.lang.String ProgramPromptsFlag
      8 digits bitmap for disabling program prompts.
      java.lang.String ReceiptPrintFlag
      Receipt printing for current command, default as “0 – No receipt”:
      java.lang.String SignatureAcquireFlag
      Whether to acquire the signature data in payment response.
      java.lang.String SignatureCaptureFlag
      The ECR supports signature printing and terminal supports signature capture, if this field exists, the value as below:
      java.lang.String SignatureUploadFlag
      Whether to upload the signature.
      java.lang.String StatusReportFlag
      Whether to report status back.
      java.lang.String TipRequestFlag
      The value is as below:
      java.lang.String UserLanguage
      Set the user language:

    • Constructor Summary

      Constructors 
      Constructor and Description
      TransactionBehavior() 

    • Method Summary

      • Methods inherited from class java.lang.Object

        clone, equals, finalize, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, toString, wait, wait, wait

    • Field Detail

      • SignatureCaptureFlag

        public java.lang.String SignatureCaptureFlag
        The ECR supports signature printing and terminal supports signature capture, if this field exists, the value as below:

        0: not capture, 1: capture.
        The default value is “0”;

        Attribute: n1

      • TipRequestFlag

        public java.lang.String TipRequestFlag
        The value is as below:

        0: not need enter tip on terminal.
        1: need enter tip on terminal.

        Attribute: n1

      • SignatureUploadFlag

        public java.lang.String SignatureUploadFlag
        Whether to upload the signature.

        “1” to upload.
        “0” not to upload.
        If this tag is not sent to POS, terminal will upload the signature by default when signature uploading is supported by host.

        Attribute: n1

      • StatusReportFlag

        public java.lang.String StatusReportFlag
        Whether to report status back.

        “1” to report.
        “0” not to report.

        Attribute: n1

      • AcceptedCardType

        public java.lang.String AcceptedCardType
        The card type bitmap, refer to Card Type Bitmap definition.
        It makes POS system available to control which card types accepted for current command. and it is mandatory for fleet transaction.

        Attribute: n32

      • ProgramPromptsFlag

        public java.lang.String ProgramPromptsFlag
        8 digits bitmap for disabling program prompts.
        The default prompt is always on and depending on the bin file. The customer can shut it off by sending in "1" in its defined program location. Refer to Program Bitmap Definition.

        Attribute: n8

      • SignatureAcquireFlag

        public java.lang.String SignatureAcquireFlag
        Whether to acquire the signature data in payment response.

        1 - Required
        0 - Not Required
        Default as 0.
        Please note that signature resolution will be reduced if SIGN data exceeds the limit when this tag is sent in Payment request.

        Attribute: n1

      • EntryMode

        public java.lang.String EntryMode
        The entry mode bitmap, refer to Entry Mode Bitmap Definition.
        It makes POS system available to control which entry mode accepted for current command.

        Attribute: n8

      • ReceiptPrintFlag

        public java.lang.String ReceiptPrintFlag
        Receipt printing for current command, default as “0 – No receipt”:

        0 – no receipt
        1 - merchant only
        2 - customer only
        3 - Both merchant /customer copy

        Attribute: n1

      • CardPresentMode

        public java.lang.String CardPresentMode
        Card Present mode for current command:

        0 - Default value, use terminal's card present setting.
        1 - Don’t prompt, assume card present.
        2 - Don’t prompt, assume card not present.
        3 – Prompt for use to select.

        Attribute: n1

      • DebitNetwork

        public java.lang.String DebitNetwork
        Indicate which debit network to use.

        1 – Star
        2 – NYCE
        3 – Pulse
        4 – Maestro
        5 – Interlink
        6 – Accel

        Attribute: n1

      • UserLanguage

        public java.lang.String UserLanguage
        Set the user language:

        1 – English
        2 – French
        3 – Chinese
        4 – Lithuanian
        5 – English UK

        Attribute: n1

      • AddlRspDataFlag

        public java.lang.String AddlRspDataFlag
        Additional response data request flag;

        0 – No
        1 – Yes
        Default to 0.

        Attribute: n1

      • ForceCC

        public java.lang.String ForceCC
        Force terminal to do commercial transaction flag:

        0 – NO
        1 – Yes
        Default to 0.

        Attribute: n1

      • ForceFsa

        public java.lang.String ForceFsa
        Force terminal to do FSA transaction flag:

        0 – No
        1 – Yes
        Default to 0.

        Attribute: n1

      • AccessibilityPinPad

        public java.lang.String AccessibilityPinPad
        The standard of accessibility to support and the status and visibility of “Accessibility Switch” on the Input Account screen.

        0 – Do not support A11Y pin pad; No switch.
        1 – On (A11Y) -Display “Accessibility Switch” in “ON” position; Use A11Y pin pad.
        2 – Off (A11Y) -Display “Accessibility Switch” in “OFF” position; Do not use A11Y pin pad.
        Empty Value/Null – Follow TMS settings for “Accessibility Switch” visibility and value.

        Attribute: n1

        Since:
        V1.10.00

      • ForceDuplicate

        public java.lang.String ForceDuplicate
        Duplicate transaction override flag, if this flag is set to ‘1’ the host will not check for duplicate, also it will override the local duplicate check. If the value is others, host dup check will be controlled by application parameter.

        Attribute: n1

      • DistransPrompts

        public java.lang.String DistransPrompts
        8 digits bitmap for disabling transaction prompts.
        The default prompt is always on and depending on the bin file.
        The customer can shut it off by sending in “1” in its defined program location.
        Refer to Prompt Bitmap Definition.

        Attribute: n24

        Since:
        V1.12.00

      • CoFIndicator

        public java.lang.String CoFIndicator
        Indicates if the transaction is Credential/Card on File (CoF) transaction.
        Flag will be ignored if the transaction doesn’t support CoF feature.

        Only one value can be set. 1 – Yes

        Attribute: n1

        Since:
        V1.14.00

      • CoFInitiator

        public java.lang.String CoFInitiator
        Indicates if the credential/card on file (CoF) transaction is customer initiated or merchant initiated.
        Utilized for subsequent CoF transactions (a token is used to process the transaction), otherwise it will be ignored for other transactions.

        C - Customer
        M - Merchant

        Attribute: a1

        Since:
        V1.14.00

      • GiftCardIndicator

        public java.lang.String GiftCardIndicator
        Gift card indicator indicates whether AMEX transactions contain a gift card purchase.

        0: Gift card not purchased
        1: Gift card purchased

        Attribute: n1

        Since:
        V1.14.00

    • Constructor Detail

      • TransactionBehavior

        public TransactionBehavior()
